The data presented in the table offers a comprehensive overview of the characteristics and functional attributes of various KLF
(Krüppel-like factor) family members, particularly focusing on their role as transcriptional repressors. The "Clusters" column
categorizes the KLFs based on their shared characteristics, while the "Sequences/Cluster" metric indicates the average number
of unique sequences associated with each cluster. Notably, the analysis identifies Group 1 KLFs, which include KLFs 3, 8, and
12, that are characterized by the presence of CtBP-binding sites. This binding capacity suggests a significant regulatory role in
transcriptional repression through their interaction with the carboxy-terminal binding protein (CtBP). The reported data also
reflects a total of 106 identified sequences associated with these KLFs, with a calculated average of 1.48 sequences per cluster,
highlighting the diverse regulatory mechanisms employed by this group. Such insights are pivotal for understanding the
molecular functions of KLFs and their implications in cellular processes, including development and disease.
